title: Active El Paso (AEP)
desc: Evaluate the health and mobility impacts of Bus Rapid Transit (BRT), examine implementation costs and benefits, and explore barriers and facilitators to BRT use.

title: Active Living Austin (ALA)
desc: Examine the causal impacts of moving to an activity-friendly community (Mueller) on residents' physical activity and health.

title: Digital Twin City for Age-friendly Communities
desc: Create a digital twin city model that identifies environmental conditions causing distress to older adults and implements interventions that promote their independent mobility and physical activity.

title: Design for Better & Safer Long-term Care
desc: Investigating how architectural design of long-term care facilities affects resident safety, physical activity, social engagement, and quality of life for older adults.

title: Healthy and Active School Design
desc: Examining how school building and campus design promotes or inhibits physical activity, active transportation, and healthy behaviors among K–12 students.

title: Intergenerational Communities Assessment Tool (iCAT)
desc: Developing and validating a community assessment tool to measure how well neighborhoods support intergenerational interactions, social cohesion, and active aging.

title: Nature-based Interventions for Healthcare Workers
desc: Studying how exposure to nature-based environments — including healing gardens and green spaces — affects stress recovery, mental health, and well-being of healthcare workers.

title: PedH (Pedestrian Health) Project
desc: Analyzing how pedestrian infrastructure, street design, and walkability features impact health outcomes, injury risk, and physical activity across diverse populations.

title: Programmable Surroundings & Their Impact on Human Behavior
desc: Exploring how smart, programmable built environments and responsive design technologies can influence health behaviors and promote active living.

title: The Green Campus Project
desc: Quantify greenery on university campuses and examine the relationship between green infrastructure and health-related outcomes among students, faculty, and staff.

title: Urban Green (Infra)structure for Pedestrian Health
desc: Develop tools and protocols for measuring urban nature in 2D/3D environments and to explore its relationship with pedestrian health outcomes such as safety, thermal comfort, mood, and stress.

title: Examining telemedicine booths as a community-based intervention to improve healthcare access in minority and underserved communities
desc: Examining how telemedicine booths can improve healthcare access in minority and underserved communities through innovative design interventions.

title: From Skepticism to adoption: Examining the application of two telehealth modalities among older adults in rural Texas
desc: Examining barriers and facilitators to telehealth adoption among older adults in rural Texas through two different modalities.

title: CALT-Project 2 Green Street
desc: Research on green street infrastructure and its impacts on community health and walkability.

title: DESIGN FOR HEALTH: Age-friendly Communities to Promote Healthy Aging in Place
desc: This research aims to identify personal and environmental barriers and facilitators of health-related outcomes among older adults living in age-targeted vs. intergenerational communities.

title: Dementia-Friendly Communities (Alzheimer Supplement)
desc: Identify environmental strategies that promote physical activity, social interaction, and independence for people with Alzheimer's Disease and Related Dementias while reducing caregiver burden.
